§6Fluxduct Tiers§f

§aTransfer RF power between machines through a tiered duct network with increasing transfer rates!§f

§bFluxducts§f carry §6Redstone Flux§f between machines. Each tier increases the maximum transfer per connection. A fluxduct network stores energy proportional to its size, buffering power across unloaded chunks.

§6Tiers§f:
§b• Leadstone§f — §e1,000 RF/tick§f. §e4× Leadstone Ingots§f → §e4× Leadstone Fluxduct§f.
§b• Hardened§f — §e4,000 RF/tick§f. §e4× Invar Ingots§f → §e4× Hardened Fluxduct§f.
§b• Redstone Energy§f — §e9,000 RF/tick§f. §e4× Electrum Ingots§f → §e4× Redstone Energy Fluxduct§f.
§b• Signalum§f — §e16,000 RF/tick§f. §e4× Signalum Ingots§f → §e4× Signalum Fluxduct§f.
§b• Resonant§f — §e25,000 RF/tick§f. §e4× Enderium Ingots§f → §e4× Resonant Fluxduct§f.
§b• Cryo-Stabilized§f — §eNo transfer limit§f. Does not store energy. Crafted using §bGelid Cryotheum§f.

§6Crafting Yield§f:
Each recipe produces §e4× fluxducts§f.

§eTip§f: Higher tiers are not always necessary — match the fluxduct to your generator output. A §e4,000 RF/tick§f dynamo does not need Signalum fluxducts.
